title: execuser: look at the source for /etc/{passwd,group} overrides
body:
look if there are bind mounts that can shadow the /etc/passwd and
/etc/group files.  In that case, look at the bind mount source.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4068#issuecomment-533782941

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

Should probably as some matching logic to ensure that if /etc and
/etc/passwd are both mounted the longest match is used

Other than @mheon's comment, LGTM, although tests aren't hip at the moment.

> Should probably as some matching logic to ensure that if /etc and /etc/passwd are both mounted the longest match is used

I think the correct behaviour is correct.  The mounts in the OCI spec file MUST be applied in the specified order (https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/blob/master/config.md#mounts), so the last one wins.
We do reorder the mounts during final spec generation, though (sorting to ensure these conflicts don't happen) - it may be after this.

> We do reorder the mounts during final spec generation, though (sorting to ensure these conflicts don't happen) - it may be after this.

I see.  Thanks for pointing it out.  I've changed it now.

Works well now:
```
$ toolbox run --release 31 printenv HOME
/home/petersen
```
